Current Cohort.name: Jeenu Pawar
Current Cohort.focus: Empowering Phase - Pardhi communities through education
Current Cohort.about: Education facilities in small villages were extremely restricted. The main impediment to rural education is uneducated parents, poor financial situation, lack of participation in education, and ignorance about educational benefits. Children and adolescents are the most affected by this condition. Shri Rudra Welfare Foundation, Nagpur creates the center in rural village which is known as "Anandghar - A Place of Joy". A spot where children will spend their time happily doing fun things and learning life skills. They openly express their emotions and thoughts to the elderly without hesitation. They want them to be happy and learning during their child time. They studied the villages and chose the most affected areas for this reason. They are launching a two-hour regular program to teach enjoyable learning education to children of various ages. They use educational fun games, social media, activity-based teaching, and other methods to achieve this goal. We provide nutritious food on a regular basis for their overall health, such as fruits, nuts, chikki, dietary food, and so on. They link the youth through this center and have a good understanding of their issues by created a youth katta for this reason, where They provide them with career advice and solve their other problems. This aided them in launching a youth-oriented career initiative.

Current Cohort.name: Sheshraj Shisodiya
Current Cohort.focus: Empowering Phase - Pardhi communities through education
Current Cohort.about: Sheshraj Shisodiya is a dedicated leader working with the Lahar Foundation in Bhopal. His focus is on providing high-quality education and building strong community support systems for children from marginalized nomadic backgrounds. Through Lahar Foundation, he has established several community learning centers that serve as safe havens for children to explore their potential and dream of a brighter future beyond their current circumstances.

Current Cohort.name: Akram
Current Cohort.focus: Empowering Phase - Pardhi communities through education
Current Cohort.about: Akram leads the Kala Samvad Foundation with a unique approach that integrates arts and dialogue into the educational experience of children. Working primarily in the Harda region, he focuses on the Pardhi community, helping children find their voices and express themselves through various creative mediums while ensuring they receive the foundational education needed to navigate the modern world with dignity and confidence.

Current Cohort.name: Ohab Ansari
Current Cohort.focus: Taking education and dignity to every doorstep of Mir, Banjara & Fakir Community
Current Cohort.about: Ohab Ansari and the Samavesh Trust are pioneers in door-to-door education for highly marginalized nomadic communities in Mehsana. His mission is to bring dignity to the doorsteps of the Mir, Banjara, and Fakir communities. By breaking down the barriers to traditional schooling, he ensures that even the most mobile populations have access to learning, healthcare, and basic rights, transforming lives one household at a time.

Current Cohort.name: Priti & Anil
Current Cohort.focus: Empowering nomadic lives through identity and education
Current Cohort.about: Priti and Anil work as a dynamic duo through the Samaj Uttan Seva Samiti to tackle the dual challenges of identity and education for nomadic tribes in Bhopal. They recognize that without formal identity documents, children are often denied access to government schools and benefits. Their work bridges this gap by helping families secure their legal identities while simultaneously running community centers that prepare children for formal schooling.

Current Cohort.name: Mehruddin
Current Cohort.focus: Smiles of learning, earning, and living well.
Current Cohort.about: Mehruddin's 'The Project Smile' in Bihar is a holistic initiative that addresses the spectrum of learning, earning, and living well. He believes that education alone is not enough if families are struggling with basic survival. His project combines community-based education for children with skill development and livelihood support for parents, creating a sustainable model of community development that keeps children in school and out of labor.
